steps.title: Compare the two dot plots
steps.content: Class A's dots sit above the integers 1 through 7, and Class B's dots sit above 14 through 20. Going column by column, the dot heights in Class A match the dot heights in Class B in the same order — the two distributions have the exact same shape.

steps.title: Apply the shift rule for standard deviation
steps.content: Class B is Class A shifted right by 13 (each value increases by the same constant). Adding a constant to every value moves the mean by that constant but leaves every deviation from the mean unchanged, so the standard deviation does not change.

steps.title: Conclude
steps.content: Same shape plus a constant shift means the two classes have equal standard deviations. That matches choice B — B is correct.
